opp.name: Stratasys_EProc+EInvoicing_April26
opp.next_step: JRA 5/20: Email to Daniel to discuss next steps with his team.

items.quote: those decision come from CFO and CEO

items.area: Single buyer-side contact role on the opportunity

metrics.narrative: Daniel Tzegay described real but unquantified pain on the invoicing side: 'there's just too many too many approval processes' and a slow, manual back-and-forth by email between suppliers, AP, and internal approvers (Avoma call, 1 May 2026). He explicitly said he wants 'one service where...everyone have visibility, there correct people approve it, done,' and referenced Workday's timestamped audit trail as the standard he is chasing. No dollar figure, hours-saved estimate, or headcount impact has been quantified yet; the move is to help Daniel build that ROI number before he takes the story to his CFO/CEO.

champion.narrative: Daniel engaged actively across the full 1 May 2026 call, asking dozens of detailed questions on invoicing, supplier risk, and negotiation AI, requesting the deck, and committing to present it to his team, director, and ultimately the CFO/CEO. That is real engagement, but there is no evidence of him advocating internally on Zycus's behalf yet, no confirmation the internal review happened, and no reply to Zycus's 20 May 2026 follow-up email in the roughly seven weeks since. He remains a promising but unproven champion candidate; his own admission that he had 'none, no familiarity with Zycus' before this call underscores how early this relationship still is.

competition.narrative: No named competitor or vendor is under active evaluation for the eProcurement/eInvoicing/Merlin scope; Daniel said he had 'none, no familiarity with Zycus' before this outreach, implying no parallel vendor conversation has surfaced. Two incumbent tools exist but sit outside current scope: Oracle (Fusion and NetSuite instances) as the ERP system of record, and Microsoft Copilot plus the Mailbox platform for CLM, on which Stratasys just signed a 10-year contract Daniel said he will not revisit. The real competitive threat at this stage is inertia and the status quo manual, email-driven invoice-approval process, not a rival vendor.

identify_pain.narrative: Daniel named two concrete pain points. First, invoicing: suppliers email invoices, and AP and internal approvers volley them back and forth by email before payment, which he called 'time consuming' and something he has 'been pushing for for quite some time' (Avoma call, 1 May 2026). Second, supplier risk: Stratasys handles DOD-related contracts and has no systematic way to screen suppliers for cyber incidents, financial distress, liens, or litigation ('does it have a lien or a judgment against the company... any litigation that might tie us and put us in jeopardy'). Both pains map directly to the eInvoicing and eProcurement/supplier-risk scope already in the deal.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed, which is expected at Qualified stage with only one buyer call on record. One relevant data point: Stratasys just signed a 10-year contract for its CLM platform (Mailbox) and does not intend to revisit it ('I don't see us moving away from that' - Daniel, 1 May 2026), so any future paper process for Zycus will exclude CLM and stay narrowly scoped to the eProcurement/eInvoicing/Merlin products already in Products__c.

economic_buyer.narrative: Daniel was explicit that he is only a recommender: 'those decision come from CFO and CEO... I have to present this out to them and see what their thoughts are before we connect again' (Avoma call, 1 May 2026). Neither the CFO nor the CEO has been named, contacted, or engaged by Zycus at any point in this record, and there is no OpportunityContactRole above Daniel's Senior Commodity Manager level. The evidence confirms budget authority sits above Daniel, but who specifically holds it, and whether they are even aware of Zycus, is unknown; the move is to help Daniel build the internal business case that gets this in front of them.

decision_process.narrative: The process as Daniel described it: he socializes internally with 'my team and my director,' then the recommendation goes up to the CFO and CEO who make the final call (Avoma call, 1 May 2026). He also referenced a hierarchy including an unnamed head of sourcing based in Israel and 'someone else in the indirect procurement hierarchy.' No formal process step (RFP, demo-to-committee, pilot) has been scheduled or confirmed since 1 May 2026, and Daniel's planned internal review ('align it sometime later on next week with my team') has never been confirmed as having happened.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria, RFP, or scorecard has surfaced in any source; the sole call to date was an introductory overview, not a criteria-gathering session. Daniel did signal directional interest areas likely to become criteria: multi-language support ('does this offer in multiple languages as we are a global company'), duplicate-PO detection against Oracle, and supplier risk screening covering cyber, financial, and litigation history for DOD-related suppliers (Avoma call, 1 May 2026). These are early signals, not confirmed criteria; the next call should convert them into a documented requirement set.

items.name: Stage: Initial Interest → Qualified
items.summary: Opportunity advanced to Qualified, but this appears procedural given the parallel health alerts showing cold engagement and no decision maker — worth confirming what actually justified the stage move.

items.name: Opportunity health review — evaluation process clarity
items.summary: Internal flag to get clarity on the buyer's evaluation stages and find ways to influence the process, suggesting we currently lack visibility into how Stratasys will actually make its decision.

items.name: Opportunity health review — org chart mapping
items.summary: Internal flag to build an org chart and map key contacts, indicating our contact coverage at Stratasys is currently too thin to navigate the account.

items.name: Opportunity health review — APD approval
items.summary: Internal flag to review and approve the Account Plan Document, a housekeeping step to keep the account plan current.

items.name: Opportunity health review — low inbound communication
items.summary: System flag noting inbound communication from the prospect has been low, reinforcing that Stratasys has gone quiet on their end.

items.name: Opportunity health review — decision maker not identified
items.summary: Internal flag confirming we still haven't pinned down who the actual decision maker is at Stratasys, a key gap given the stage advancement.

items.name: Opportunity health review — multi-threading
items.summary: Internal reminder to keep engagement multi-threaded rather than relying on a single contact, likely prompted by the thin org chart.

items.name: Opportunity health review — identify more contact roles
items.summary: Internal flag to identify additional contact roles within Stratasys, consistent with the broader theme of insufficient account mapping.

items.name: Opportunity health review — prospect gone cold
items.summary: System alert that there's been no inbound communication from Stratasys in 14 days, the clearest signal that buyer engagement has stalled.

items.name: Outbound follow-up reminder
items.summary: System-generated nudge noting 10 days have passed without an outbound email, prompting the rep to send a follow-up to re-engage the account.

day_summary.overall: Stratasys was bumped from Initial Interest to Qualified, but the health-check flags tell a more cautious story than the stage change suggests: no inbound from the prospect in two weeks, no outbound email in ten days, and the decision maker still isn't identified. This looks like an internal process/scoring update rather than genuine buyer momentum — the deal is stalled on engagement and needs to be re-threaded before it can be called truly qualified.

cro_panel.intro: Stratasys is a single-threaded, early-stage evaluation; Daniel Tzegay liked the eInvoicing and supplier-risk story on the 1 May 2026 intro call but has gone quiet for over two months, and the real decision sits with an unengaged CFO/CEO he has not yet briefed

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 9 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 29 is carried by differentiation strength +0.85 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'inefficien' in…; recent CRM moves: stage advanced. Offsetting it: momentum 41 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 41 (slipping backward): last genuine buyer touch 68d ago vs the ~30d cadence this stage needs — going quiet. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.15).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 28 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 1 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 19 is anchored on the stage win (29), nudged by commitment 28 and momentum 41, and dragged by risk 9. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: a single strong discovery call was followed by seven weeks of silence despite a logged rep follow-up.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-07; close_date 2026-09-01 = 56 days out. Opportunity created 2026-05-01 (same day as the sole substantive buyer call), so the deal has spent all ~67 days of its life in Qualified with no reported advance to Formal Evaluation. LastActivityDate is 2026-05-01 (67 days ago); Next_Step__c logs a rep follow-up email on 2026-05-20 (48 days ago) with no reply on record since. At this pace, and with the economic buyer (CFO/CEO) never engaged, the deal cannot realistically reach Shortlisted/Vendor Selected before the 1 Sep 2026 close date without an immediate re-engagement.
evidence: Only one substantive buyer call on record (1 May 2026); no further engagement logged since.
Rep follow-up email sent 20 May 2026 (Next_Step__c) has no logged reply after roughly seven weeks.
Daniel Tzegay stated the decision rests with the CFO and CEO, neither of whom has been contacted or named.
Close date is 56 days out while the deal sits at Qualified stage with no economic buyer engagement.
north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded Qualified deal, cooling for 67 days since its only real discovery call; the 1 Sep 2026 close date is not achievable without an immediate re-engagement and access to the CFO/CEO who hold the decision.

flags: Multi-thread beyond Daniel Tzegay: get his director and the Israel-based sourcing lead into a call before the deal advances past Qualified.

The status quo manual, email-driven invoice-approval process is the real competitor to displace; no named eProcurement/eInvoicing/Merlin rival has been raised in any source.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Stratasys is a single-threaded, early-stage evaluation: Daniel Tzegay liked the eInvoicing and supplier-risk story on the 1 May 2026 intro call but has gone quiet for over two months, and the real decision sits with an unengaged CFO/CEO he has not yet briefed.

deal_risk.text: With a 1 Sep 2026 close date on record and the deal still at Qualified with no economic buyer engaged, the current close date is not realistically achievable without a fast re-engagement.

win_position.text: Daniel Tzegay engaged deeply on the 1 May 2026 call, asking detailed questions on AP Smartdesk invoicing automation and supplier risk screening for DOD-related suppliers, and asked Zycus to send the deck for internal review.
The real budget decision rests with the CFO and CEO, per Daniel's own words, and neither has been engaged or named on this deal.
Daniel said he'd regroup with his team and reconnect in 'a couple weeks' on 1 May 2026, but there has been no confirmed reply since, even after a follow-up email logged 20 May 2026.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-facing engagement has been recorded in the 67 days since the only substantive call (1 May 2026), and the account is still single-threaded through Daniel alone.

customer_commitment.text: Daniel has not yet looped in his director, team, or the CFO/CEO as he said he would on the 1 May 2026 call; that internal socialization step remains unconfirmed.

CLM overlap with the existing Copilot/Mailbox stack was explicitly ruled out of scope by Daniel.

hard.ais_why:
• Prospect is actively pitching Merlin Intake and AP Smart Desk internally, planning to present the concept to CFO/CEO as part of his recommendation.
• Customer asked for the deck plus screenshots, specifically calling out Merlin Intake and supplier risk radar examples.
• Pain points center on reducing manual, email-based AP/invoice approvals and automating small/tail spend sourcing. Automation theme lines up with AP Smart Desk and ANA value props.
